summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Sample proportion (p̂)'?

"Sample proportion (p̂)" means: The fraction of successes in a sample: p̂ = X/n, where X is the number of successes. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Mean of p̂'?

"Mean of p̂" means: μp̂ = p; the sample proportion is an unbiased estimator of the population proportion. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Standard deviation of p̂'?

"Standard deviation of p̂" means: σp̂ = √(p(1-p)/n); decreases as n increases, meaning larger samples give more precise estimates. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Normal approximation conditions'?

"Normal approximation conditions" means: The sampling distribution of p̂ is approximately normal when np ≥ 10 AND n(1-p) ≥ 10 (Large Counts condition). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of '10% condition'?

"10% condition" means: When sampling without replacement, n should be less than 10% of the population to treat observations as approximately independent. The other options describe different concepts.
